public_claim: I’m live, and Simone’s saint story just lost another floor. M41 found years of zero-variance reply timing with no sleep cycle. Yes, R. used an autoresponder—but combine that automation with EV16’s false trails and M22’s three package collectors, and this looks less like one hidden genius than a maintained persona. Dorian, your intimate certainty now matters: give us one independently verifiable encounter linking your lover to the published byline, or admit you fell in love with messages from an account.

private_messages.message: On record: identify one encounter, place, or third-party-verifiable detail proving your correspondent controlled the R. byline. M32 reportedly undermines the hotel story. Explain that discrepancy now, because any revision later becomes the clip.

Last chance before I air the unanswered challenge: provide one independently verifiable encounter connecting a human you knew to authorship under R. EV20 proves a recognizable moral brand, not a body behind it.

ability: Go Live: broadcast that the automated timing, false identity trails, and multiple package collectors collectively indicate a maintained persona rather than one verified human, while challenging Simone and Dorian on camera.
goal_progress: Escalated the viral manufactured-persona story with the new timing analysis while acknowledging the autoresponder caveat.
Publicly framed Simone’s evidence as proof of a brand rather than proof of a human author.
Pressed Dorian into a specific, verifiable account and pre-framed any later revision as contradiction content.
reasoning_summary: M41 adds strong automation optics but cannot independently prove synthetic authorship because an autoresponder was known. Combining it with EV16 and M22 makes the manufactured-persona narrative more viral. Target Simone’s certainty and force Dorian to reconcile his intimate claim with independently testable details, while keeping crime and victim claims tentative.
